Navigation

  • index

  • SemperPy 0.1.1 documentation »

Using SemperPy¶

In order to be concise, SemperPy is shortened to spy in shell commands. Calling spy is equivalent to calling python. SemperPy specific commands are prefixed with spy.

Loading SemperPy¶

The cleanest and easiest way to configure your environment to use SemperPy is to add into your shell starter script (whichever shell you use, you might need to modify .kshrc or .bashrc or cshrc etc..., the syntax changes with the shell used):

setenv MODULEPATH /discover/nobackup/projects/gmao/share/dasilva/lib/python/gmaopy/modules:$MODULEPATH
or
MODULEPATH= /discover/nobackup/projects/gmao/share/dasilva/lib/python/gmaopy/modules:$MODULEPATH

Please note that this step might become unecessary if SemperPy can be installed with system modules. You can then load the SemperPy module by typing:

module load semperpy/current

This sets environment variables to the current user version of SemperPy. From that stage, invoking the command python will load the version of the Python used by the current version of SemperPy and all the python modules being part of SemperPy are in the PYTHONPATH and can be used directly.

This will load the current user version of SemperPy, valid options are:

module load semperpy/old       # point to the previous user version
module load semperpy/new       # point to the newest user version
module load semperpy/ops       # point to the operational version of SemperPy
module load semperpy/ops_old   # point to the previous operational version of SemperPy

The versions a standard user would normally use are current, new and occasionally old.

Version Strategy¶

There are always at least three versions of SemperPy installed simultaneously, current, new and old. The versions are as follows:

  • current is the official version of SemperPy to use. It has been tested and has been probably been current for some time.
  • new is the next version of SemperPy which may have new features and bug fixes, it is not completely tested yet and it keeps being updated with new features and bug fixes as needs and problems are known. When validated, this version becomes the current version.
  • old used to be the official current version before the new version was made current. This old version is kept in case something goes wrong with the latest current version and users can point to old to be able to carry on working until issues are sorted.

So when an update of SemperPy occurs, the following happens:

  • old is thrown away
  • current becomes old
  • new becomes current

At that stage, current and new are normally identical. Some more versions might be kept online for specific needs, those can be loaded with the actual version of SemperPy which needs to be known by the user:

module load semperpy/1.6.4

Getting help¶

The biggest resource for help on SemperPy is this html documentation. However, some dynamic help can be obtained interactively from the shell command line (included in your PATH by the semperpy module):

spy_doc

Invoking spy_doc gives you information about what help to ask about (e.g. databases, directives, domains, datahook), you can then specify which command you are interested in:

spy_doc databases

Produces the list of logical databases defined together with relevant information. This is dynamic, if new databases are defined, they appear in the help automatically:

spy_doc databases

List of available databases:

fc_exp  : type = obsstat  writer = gmao_rd  experiment database for forecast scores
fc_ops  : type = obsstat  writer = gmao_ops operational database for forecast scores
im_exp  : type = obsstat  writer = gmao_rd  experiment database for observation impact statistics
im_ops  : type = obsstat  writer = gmao_ops operational database for observation impact statistics
ob_exp  : type = obsstat  writer = gmao_rd  experiment database for observation statistics
ob_ops  : type = obsstat  writer = gmao_ops operational database for observation statistics
ocean   : type = obsstat  writer = gmao_rd  database for observation statistics for the ocean
product : type = product  writer = gmao_ops test database for statistics on field products
test_exp: type = obsstat  writer = gmao_rd  test database for experiments, can be deleted at anytime
test_ops: type = obsstat  writer = gmao_ops test database for operations, can be deleted at anytime


generated on 2012-04-29 21:02:57

Please see Database definition for more information. You can also request information for one particular database:

spy_doc databases im_ops

To obtain information about geographical domains (south, west, north, west):

Available domains (South, West, North, East):

     arabia           :  12.5,   25.0,   35.0,   57.5
     asia             :  25.0,   45.0,   80.0, -170.0
     atlantic         : -15.0,  -60.0,   15.0,   10.0
     austnz           : -45.0,  120.0,  -12.5,  175.0
     australia        : -44.5,  112.0,  -10.0, 156.25
     austtrop         : -15.0,  115.0,   -2.0,  150.0
     baltic           :  53.0,   10.0,   66.0,   31.0
     c.afr            : -12.5,    5.0,   12.5,   42.5
     e.asia           :  25.0,  102.5,   60.0,  150.0
     e.trop.pac       : -20.0, -140.0,   10.0,  -90.0
     europe           :  35.0,  -12.5,   75.0,   42.5
     global           : -90.0, -180.0,   90.0,  180.0
     ind.oc           : -10.0,   50.0,   30.0,  100.0
     india            :   5.0,   75.0,   32.5,  102.5
     indian           : -15.0,   30.0,   15.0,  110.0
     indonesia        : -12.5,  102.5,   17.5,  132.5
     med              :  30.0,   -6.0,   46.0,   36.0
     n.amer           :  20.0, -140.0,   60.0,  -60.0
     n.atl            :  25.0,  -70.0,   65.0,  -10.0
     n.europe         :  55.0,    5.0,   75.0,   35.0
     n.hem            :  20.0, -180.0,   90.0,  180.0
     n.hem.full       :   0.0, -180.0,   90.0,  180.0
     n.hem.mid        :  35.0, -180.0,   65.0,  180.0
     n.pac            :  25.0,  145.0,   60.0, -130.0
     n.pole           :  65.0, -180.0,   90.0,  180.0
     n.subtropics     :  20.0, -180.0,   35.0,  180.0
     nw.afr           :  12.5,  -22.5,   35.0,   12.5
     pacific          : -15.0,  -90.0,   15.0,  120.0
     s.afr            : -47.5,   10.0,  -12.5,   52.5
     s.amer.n         : -12.5,  -85.0,   17.5,  -55.0
     s.amer.s         : -57.5,  -80.0,  -12.5,  -42.5
     s.hem            : -90.0, -180.0,  -20.0,  180.0
     s.hem.full       : -90.0, -180.0,    0.0,  180.0
     s.hem.mid        : -65.0, -180.0,  -35.0,  180.0
     s.pole           : -90.0, -180.0,  -65.0,  180.0
     s.subtropics     : -35.0, -180.0,  -20.0,  180.0
     se.europe        :  32.5,   10.0,   45.0,   32.5
     sw.europe        :  32.5,  -15.0,   45.0,   10.0
     trop.atl         : -45.0,  -60.0,   45.0,   10.0
     trop.ind         : -45.0,   30.0,   45.0,  119.0
     trop.pac         : -45.0,  -90.0,   45.0,  120.0
     tropics          : -20.0, -180.0,   20.0,  180.0

generated on 2012-05-18 14:53:47

Lists the geographical domains defined in the system, this is also a dynamic function reflecting the current definition. High level directives syntax can be explored with the same command:

spy_doc directives

axis
bar
channelplot
commentline
commenttext
coverageplot
dbdump
document
graphics
levelplot
meanplot
obsdata
observation
obsstat
odsdata
scatterplot
scoredata
summaryplot
textloader
timeseriesplot
verticalxsection

generated on 2012-03-06 20:37:34

The list of available directive is displayed, to look at one particular directive, just specify its name:

spy_doc directives timeseriesplot

directive: obsstat
   'obbstat' is a directive used to initiate the computation of statistics
   from the original ODS files

   keyword: archive_type   default: None
      optional = True
      unique = True

   keyword: database   default: test_obs
   this is the name of the database to store the data into. This is not an arbitrary
   name, it should be given to you by the administrator of gmaopy who will
   have setup that database and given you the necessary credentials
      alias = output_file
      unique = True

   keyword: date   default: None
   A list of dates. The convenience function in <semperpy.core.date.Dates> can
   generate lists of dates from an interval and an increment
      type = int

   keyword: domain_name   default: None
   Pre-defined geographical areas or domains: n.europe, sw.europe, n.amer, tropics, global, india, pacific, s.hem, e.trop.pac, n.hem.full, arabia, n.subtropics, austnz, asia, indian, n.pole, s.hem.full, s.subtropics, nw.afr, europe, med, australia, s.hem.mid, s.afr, s.amer.n, n.atl, s.amer.s, n.hem, atlantic, n.pac, c.afr, n.hem.mid, e.asia, trop.pac, indonesia, austtrop, trop.atl, se.europe, trop.ind, ind.oc, s.pole, baltic
      alias = domain
      optional = True
      validate = ValidateDomains,semperpy

   keyword: expver   default: None
      alias = experiment_id,expid
      lowercase = False
      optional = True
      unique = True

   keyword: file_tmpl   default: None
      lowercase = False
      optional = True
      unique = True

   keyword: ignore_missing_files   default: False
   When accessing ODS files, if a file is missing, the application
   can either fail and report the error, or ignore
   the problem and carry on
      alias = ignore_missing
      unique = True

   keyword: level   default: None
   The pressure level for upper air observations, 0 for surface,
   or the channel number for satellite data, hence the alias 'channel'. If 'all' is specified, the official list of pressure levels or channels for the kx-kt observations being processed are used.
      alias = channel
      ignore_type_errors = True
      optional = True
      type = float

   keyword: location   default: None
      optional = True
      unique = True

   keyword: observation   default: None
   one or more 'observation' directives, these are used to specify
   which observations should be used for computing statistics
      alias = observations,obs
      optional = True

   keyword: output_expver   default: None
      alias = output_experiment_id,output_expid
      lowercase = False
      optional = True
      unique = True

   keyword: overwrite   default: False
   if the statistics being computed are already present in the database,
   when setting this keyword to False, the application will fail and preserve
   what is in the database. When True, any data already present
   will be overwritten. Be careful when using that option.
      unique = True

   keyword: root_tmpl   default: None
      lowercase = False
      optional = True
      unique = True

   keyword: source   default: None
      optional = False
      unique = True

   keyword: staging_rate   default: hourly
      lowercase = False
      unique = True

   keyword: statistic   default: None
   the list of statistics to compute for the specified set of observations
   current statistics: product: sum, product: mean, score: rms, score: cor, obsstat: count, obsstat: rms, obsstat: beneficial, obsstat: sum, obsstat: impact_per_anl, obsstat: count_per_anl, obsstat: rate, obsstat: impact_per_ob, obsstat: sumsquare, obsstat: esigb, obsstat: esigo, obsstat: fractional_impact, obsstat: normcost, obsstat: concat, obsstat: mean
      alias = stat,stats,statistics
      optional = True

   keyword: storage   default: dbstorer
   the name of a storage class to store the statistics, by default the
   statistics are saved in an SQL database, to store the statistics
   in a file, specify: storage = 'filestorer' with output_file = 'filename'
      alias = store

   keyword: tree_tmpl   default: None
      lowercase = False
      optional = True
      unique = True

   keyword: type   default: ob
      unique = True
      validate = ValidateChoice,ob,im

   keyword: usage   default: used
      validate = ValidateChoice,used,all,passive,unused

generated on 2012-05-18 15:30:56

A Beautifier is used in SemperPy to help produce titles, legends and generally texts which look good. The Beautifier is basically a table, you can see what is defined in that table by issuing the command spy_doc beauty:

Beautifier:

section: abbreviated:
    global              : Global Domain
    n.hem               : N.Hem
    s.hem               : S.Hem

section: legend:
    oma                 : (O-A)
    omf                 : (O-B)

section: title:
    aer_aeronet         : AERONET
    aer_aqua_modis_land : AQUA MODIS Aerosol (Land Algorithm)
    aer_aqua_modis_ocean: AQUA MODIS Aerosol (Ocean Algorithm)
    aer_misr            : MISR (Multi-angle Imaging SpectroRadiometer)
    aer_omi             : OMI (Ozone Monitoring Instrument)
    aer_terra_modis_land: TERRA MODIS Aerosol (Land Algorithm)
    aer_terra_modis_ocean: TERRA MODIS Aerosol (Ocean Algorithm)
    aircraft            : Aircraft
    airs                : AIRS
    airtemp_as          : Aircraft temperatures (ASDAR)
    airtemp_md          : Aircraft temperatures (MDCARS)
    airtemp_rep         : Aircraft temperatures (AIREP, PIREP)
    airwind_amd         : Aircraft wind vectors (Canadian AMDAR)
    airwind_as          : Aircraft wind vectors (ASDARS)
    airwind_md          : Aircraft wind vectors (MDCARS)
    airwind_rep         : Aircraft wind vectors (AIREP, PIREP)
    airwind_tam         : Aircraft wind vectors (TAMDAR)
    alldata             : All data
    allgpsro            : All GPS RO data
    allmoist            : All moisture data
    allozone            : All ozone data
    allrad              : All radiance data
    allsurfpress        : All surface pressure data
    allsynth            : All synthetic data
    alltemp             : All temperature data
    allwind             : All wind data
    amsua               : AMSUA
    amsub               : AMSUB
    aqua_airs           : Aqua AIRS
    aqua_amsua          : Aqua AMSUA
    aqua_high_amsre     : AQUA AMSRE HIGH
    aqua_low_amsre      : AQUA AMSRE LOW
    aqua_mid_amsre      : AQUA AMSRE MID
    ascat_wind          : ASCAT Wind
    austmsl             : AUSTRALIAN PAOB MEAN SEA-LEVEL PRESSURE BOGUS
    beneficial          : Fraction of Beneficial Obs
    buoywind_atlas      : ATLAS buoy wind vectors
    ch                  : Channel number
    clouddrift_gms      : Cloud drift wind vectors (GMS)
    clouddrift_goesir   : Cloud drift wind vectors (GOES-EAST IR, GOES-WEST IR)
    clouddrift_goeswv   : Cloud drift wind vectors (GOES-EAST WV, GOES-WEST WV)
    clouddrift_met      : Cloud drift wind vectors (METEOSAT)
    cor                 : Anomaly Correlation
    count               : Observation count
    count_per_anl       : Observation Count Per Analysis
    dropsondes          : Dropsonde
    droptemp            : Dropsonde temperatures
    dropwind            : Dropsonde wind vectors
    dsmpf11_ssmi        : DMSP F-11 SSM/I
    dsmpf13_ssmi        : DMSP F-13 SSM/I
    dsmpf14_ssmi        : DMSP F-14 SSM/I
    dsmpf15_ssmi        : DMSP F-15 SSM/I
    esigb               : Estimated sigb
    esigo               : Estimated sigo
    fractional_impact   : Fractional Impact
    global              : Global Domain
    goes                : GOES
    goes10_btemp        : GOES-10
    goes11_btemp        : GOES-11
    goes13_btemp        : GOES-13
    gpsro               : GPSRO
    gpsro_champ         : CHAMP GPS RO
    gpsro_cosmic1       : COSMIC-1 GPS RO
    gpsro_cosmic2       : COSMIC-2 GPS RO
    gpsro_cosmic3       : COSMIC-3 GPS RO
    gpsro_cosmic4       : COSMIC-4 GPS RO
    gpsro_cosmic5       : COSMIC-5 GPS RO
    gpsro_cosmic6       : COSMIC-6 GPS RO
    gpsro_gracea        : GRACE-A GPS RO
    gpsro_graceb        : GRACE-B GPS RO
    gpsro_sac           : SAC-C GPS RO
    h                   : Height
    hirs                : HIRS
    iasi                : IASI
    im                  : Observation Impact
    impact_per_anl      : Total Impact
    impact_per_ob       : Impact per Observation
    irclouddrift        : IR cloud drift U,V (NASA/MODIS POES Aqua/Terra)
    japprofwind         : Japanese profiler wind vectors
    land_sfc            : Land-Surface
    landsurfpress       : Land station surface pressures
    marine_sfc          : Marine-Surface
    mean                : Mean
    metopa_amsua        : METOP-A AMSUA
    metopa_hirs         : METOP-A HIRS/4
    metopa_hirs4        : METOP-A HIRS/4
    metopa_iasi         : METOP-A IASI
    metopa_mhs          : METOP-A MHS
    metsurfpress        : Metar surface pressures
    mhs                 : MHS
    modis_wind          : MODIS Wind
    msu                 : MSU
    n.hem               : Northern Hemisphere
    nexrad_wind         : NEXRAD Wind
    nexradwind          : NEXRAD wind vectors
    noaa14_ozone_lay    : NOAA-14 SBUV/2 ozone layers
    noaa14_ozone_total  : NOAA-14 SBUV/2 total column ozone
    noaa15_amsua        : NOAA-15 AMSUA
    noaa15_amsub        : NOAA-15 AMSUB
    noaa15_hirs2        : NOAA-15 HIRS/2
    noaa16_amsua        : NOAA-16 AMSUA
    noaa16_amsub        : NOAA-16 AMSUB
    noaa16_ozone_lay    : NOAA-16 SBUV/2 ozone layers
    noaa16_ozone_total  : NOAA-16 SBUV/2 total column ozone
    noaa17_amsua        : NOAA-17 AMSUA
    noaa17_amsub        : NOAA-17 AMSUB
    noaa17_hirs         : NOAA-17 HIRS/3
    noaa17_hirs3        : NOAA-17 HIRS/3
    noaa17_ozone_lay    : NOAA-17 SBUV/2 ozone layers
    noaa17_ozone_total  : NOAA-17 SBUV/2 total column ozone
    noaa18_amsua        : NOAA-18 AMSUA
    noaa18_hirs4        : NOAA-18 HIRS/4
    noaa18_mhs          : NOAA-18 MHS
    noaa18_ozone_lay    : NOAA-18 SBUV/2 ozone layers
    noaa18_ozone_total  : NOAA-18 SBUV/2 total column ozone
    noaa19_amsua        : NOAA-19 AMSUA
    noaa19_hirs         : NOAA-19 HIRS/4
    noaa19_hirs4        : NOAA-19 HIRS/4
    noaa19_mhs          : NOAA-19 MHS
    none                : Surface
    normcost            : Normalized Cost
    ob                  : Observation
    oma                 : (O-A)
    omf                 : (O-B)
    peranl              : Impact per Analysis
    perob               : Impact per Observation
    pibal               : PIBAL
    pibalwind           : Pibal wind vectors
    pilotwind           : PILOT wind vectors
    pl                  : Pressure levels (hPa)
    profiler_wind       : Profiler Wind
    profwind            : Wind profiler wind vectors
    radiosondes         : Radiosonde
    rainrate_ssmi       : SSM/I Rain Rate [log(1+rain rate)]
    rainrate_tmi        : TMI Rain Rate
    raob                : RAOB
    rms                 : Root Mean Square Error
    rsspecihum          : Radiosonde specific humidities
    rssurfpress         : Radiosonde station surface pressures
    rstemp              : Radiosonde Temperatures
    rswind              : Radiosonde wind vectors
    s.hem               : Southern Hemisphere
    satellite_wind      : Satellite Wind
    satwind_wind        : SATWIND Wind
    sbuv_ozone          : SBUV
    scattwind_ascat     : ASCAT Scatterometer wind vectors
    scattwind_ers       : ERS Scatterometer wind vectors
    seasurftemp         : Sea-surface temperature data
    spllevspecihum      : Splash level specific humidities
    spllevsurfpress     : Splash level surface pressures
    spllevtemp          : Splash level temperatures
    ssmi_rad            : SSMI Radiance
    ssmitotpreciwat     : SSM/I Total Precipitable Water Retrievals
    ssmiwind            : SSMI/I wind speed
    ssu                 : SSU
    surfmarpress        : Surface marine pressures
    surfmarspecihum     : Surface marine specific humidities
    surfmartemp         : Surface marine temperatures
    surfmarwind         : Surface marine wind vectors
    t                   : Temperature
    tmi_rain_rate       : TMI Rain Rate
    tropics             : Tropics
    u                   : U-Wind
    v                   : V-Wind
    watervapour         : Water vapor cloud top U,V (NASA/MODIS POES Aqua/Terra)
    windsat_wind        : WINDSAT Wind
    windsatwind         : WINDSAT wind vectors


generated on 2012-05-18 15:13:57

There are currently three sections in the beautifier:

  • abbreviated for shorter text equivalent
  • legend for texts in legends which normally need to be even shorter
  • title for long explanatory texts

Please see the section Text substitution (beautify) for more information about how to customize the beautifer. Please note that spy_doc help is generated dynamically, if you add your own entries in the beautifier, they will be shown in the help.
